nodes.label: Summary
nodes.summary: Realizing efficient immobilization of lithium polysulfides (LiPSs) as well as reversible catalytic conversion between LiPSs and the insoluble Li 2 S is vital to restrain the shuttle effect, which requires highly reactive catalysts for high-performance Li-S batteries.

evidence.excerpt: Realizing efficient immobilization of lithium polysulfides (LiPSs) as well as reversible catalytic conversion between LiPSs and the insoluble Li 2 S is vital to restrain the shuttle effect, which requires highly reactive catalysts for high-performance Li-S batteries.

Introduction
Conclusion

Realizing efficient immobilization of lithium polysulfides (LiPSs) as well as reversible catalytic conversion between LiPSs and the insoluble Li 2 S is vital to restrain the shuttle effect, which requires highly reactive catalysts for high-performance Li-S batteries.

summary.contentSummary: Realizing efficient immobilization of lithium polysulfides (LiPSs) as well as reversible catalytic conversion between LiPSs and the insoluble Li 2 S is vital to restrain the shuttle effect, which requires highly reactive catalysts for high-performance Li-S batteries.
